applied_paragraph: the owner builds systems (Example Health App, Brain wiki) where measurement discipline matters. The benchmark-as-hypothesis framing maps directly onto how to evaluate whether new pipeline stages are working — don't trust unit test coverage alone, run the pipeline in conditions that match production.

applied_bullets.claim: Synthetic tests can hide emergent production failure modes
applied_bullets.why_it_matters: The Brain wiki pipeline has caching and idempotency logic that only breaks under real multi-run conditions
applied_bullets.action: Add integration smoketests that exercise the full pipeline against fixture data

applied_bullets.claim: Tail latency matters more than mean throughput at scale
applied_bullets.why_it_matters: Gemini API calls in the pipeline are not uniformly fast — worst-case latency determines perceived reliability
applied_bullets.action: Log and monitor p95/p99 Gemini call latency, not just averages

applied_bullets.why_it_matters: Profiling the enrichment pipeline changes its memory and timing behavior
applied_bullets.action: Profile in staging, not in production runs

socratic_questions: How would you know if the Brain wiki pipeline's caching logic is broken in a way that only surfaces after many sequential runs?
What is the production equivalent of 'shadow traffic' for a personal knowledge base?
Are the integration tests you're writing right now benchmarks in the misleading sense — isolated from real ingest conditions?
